author: bharath.vedartham
content:
I am working on implementing the builder-api in buildoor and prototyping it in prysm. I want to raise a point:
When we are proposing a block, the validator client calls the beacon node to get the beacon block, the validator client signs the block, and then sends the signed block back to the beacon node to broadcast it. 
When the signed block is sent to the beacon node, this is when beacon nodes check whether to call the `getPayload` API to the builder. This is called if the block is blinded which is easy to check pre gloas. 
Post gloas, this will get hard cause you cannot distinguish if a bid is from the builder api or from the p2p.

author: bharath.vedartham
content: In my prysm prototype, b/w both the calls I maintain a cache b/w block root -> bid type where I mark whether the bid is from the builder-api or not. But I am not sure if this is the correct solution

author: nflaig
content: for builder api purposes, don't you even wanna "remember" from which builder (based on url/or index) you got the bid?

author: bharath.vedartham
content: yeah exactly, cause today we can call the `getPayload` API to all relays and its fine but with ePBS, we need to send the beacon block back only to the winning builder

author: bharath.vedartham
content: because only they can reveal the payload

author: nflaig
content: let's get rid of this api altogether maybe? technically it shouldn't be needed, they can observe that block via p2p

author: bharath.vedartham
content: here we will rely on proposers doing all work to broadcast the block, maybe thats fine? I kept the endpoint because I thought even the builder can help in block broadcast in this case

author: bharath.vedartham
content: today the relays also help in broadcasting the block as we assume that these guys have really good n/w infra. But today we also have the full payload in the block, maybe this api doesn't matter hmm

author: bharath.vedartham
content: i also don't think a serious builder will reveal the block immediately as you probably want the block to gain enough attestations before revealing the payload. I think there is a good reason to not have the API

author: jameshe5018
content: i approved https://github.com/ethereum/beacon-APIs/pull/612#pullrequestreview-4413024578 but waiting for more opinions

author: nflaig
content: we need to add a note (ideally not written by claude) that explains what 404 means

author: nc1234
content: Added a note

author: nflaig
content:
the spec says the following
> If the validator has not seen any beacon block for the assigned slot, do not submit a payload attestation; it will be ignored anyway.
so "canonical block" seems wrong, <@884836500037066862> does prysm properly implement this, I think our implementation is not correct

author: jameshe5018
content: the 404? we don't

author: nflaig
content: no, I mean canonical block for slot vs. any block for slot

author: nflaig
content: either way, the spec and beacon-api PR wording do not match

author: nc1234
content: I think this needs spec clarification, whether a PTC should cast payload attestation for a non-canonical block. If there are both canonical and non-canonical block received in the same slot, which one should PTC cast its payload attestation to

author: jameshe5018
content: need to double check, but there's a likely hood it's not will ask internally though

author: nflaig
content: you pick the one with more weight in that case?

author: nc1234
content: I think so. PTC should just pick the block with the most weight, canonical or not. I think it's beneficial for the network to allow PTC cast votes for non-canonical block because it is possible there is a reorg next slot, and having timlieness and data availability info helps.

author: nflaig
content: in case of beacon block reorg the ptc isn't relevant, but the block can still become canonical and ptc votes can be relevant, so I think any, as in the spec is the right wording

author: nc1234
content: Updated the PR

author: jameshe5018
content: ok cool sounds good, yeah i think any block is right, didn't look carefully enough

author: nflaig
content: I would prefer to have the 2nd part "Used to signal validator to not cast any payload attestation." but rephrased a bit differently in the description

author: jameshe5018
content: do you want me to remove it from the pr for now? we can add it back in if needed later, just wanted to move this pr along

author: nc1234
content: Do you have access to my branch? You can just edit directly as you please 😆

author: nflaig
content: I can fix it tomorrow and merge it, we also need to fix our implementation

author: nc1234
content: Yes I will do it on lodestar

author: nflaig
content: so prysm does vote for non canonical blocks too, seems to match the spec wording
